differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer with localized retroperitoneal abscess and duodeno-renal fistula
S. anginosus bacteremia secondary to occult retroperitoneal source
next_steps: Perform urgent esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) to evaluate duodenal pathology and rule out fistula/malignancy
Perform transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to definitively exclude endocarditis
Repeat abdominal imaging (CT or MRI) with sedation to better characterize the duodeno-renal connection and rule out hidden abscesses
Obtain Strongyloides IgG serology and stool ova and parasites (O&P) testing
Continue systemic anticoagulation with close monitoring for GI bleeding given the duodenal findings
Consult Interventional Radiology for potential drainage of the peri-duodenal or pararenal space
Continue targeted antibiotic therapy for S. anginosus while monitoring for clinical response
